summary: Great audience tape with stellar Eyes‑of‑the‑World/Wharf Rat horns and solid playing, marred only by a muffled China Cat→Rider jam.
review: The GEMS audience recording captures a vibrant September ’73 show highlighted by an inspired Eyes of the World into Wharf Rat sequence and strong horn work, especially on the later tracks. While the overall sound is crisp and the performance solid, the China Cat Sunflower → The Rider jam suffers from an alternate muffled source that detracts from an otherwise excellent tape.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise the musical performance and overall recording quality, citing standout songs and good sound, with only a few notes about muffled patches and an average impression from one reviewer.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally crisp audience tape with excellent blend; however the China Cat→Rider jam is taken from a muffled alternate source and there is a slight echoy character in parts of the set.

poor_songs: China Cat Sunflower > The Rider (Feelin' Groovy Jam)
show_quality.setlist_flow: The set flows well with a strong horn‑driven middle section and solid overall playing, but the muffled China Cat→Rider segment interrupts the momentum.

band_member_comments.Keys: Keith is on fire; keyboards receive high praise.
